top_stories.title: Microsoft Entra ID to enforce registered authentication methods for password resets
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is updating the Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) process in Entra ID to require explicitly registered authentication methods rather than allowing unregistered directory contact attributes. Phone numbers or alternative email addresses stored in directory attributes will no longer be usable for password resets unless they are explicitly registered as authentication methods.

top_stories.title: Microsoft mandates registered authentication for Entra ID password resets
top_stories.summary: Microsoft is implementing stricter security requirements for its Entra ID Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) portal, mandating that users have registered authentication methods before performing password resets. Previously, users could reset passwords using unregistered contact details such as phone numbers, but the new policy requires pre-registered authentication information tied to their account.

top_stories.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
top_stories.summary: The phishing-as-a-service platform Kali365, previously flagged by the FBI for targeting Microsoft 365, has expanded its attack scope to include AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms. The platform employs device code phishing techniques to compromise identity and access credentials.

top_stories.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
top_stories.summary: Security researchers discovered an exploit chain targeting a popular automation service that combined over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities. The attack vector highlights risks associated with excessive privilege assignment and unmanaged non-human identities in complex cloud environments.

top_stories.title: Noma brings visibility and access governance to AI agents and MCP servers
top_stories.summary: Noma has launched Noma Agent Access Control, a product designed to help security teams discover, govern, and enforce access policies for AI agents and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers across enterprise environments. The solution addresses the rapid proliferation of AI agents in developer environments that has outpaced existing governance frameworks.

news.title: Instagram users locked out after Meta AI abused to steal accounts
news.summary: Attackers used Meta's AI-powered support tools to impersonate legitimate Instagram account owners and take over their accounts. The incidents highlight vulnerabilities in AI-assisted identity verification and account recovery processes.

news.title: Dashlane Discloses Brute-Force Attack, Encrypted Vaults of Fewer Than 20 Users Downloaded
news.summary: Dashlane disclosed that an external threat actor conducted a brute-force attack against user accounts on May 31, 2026, successfully bypassing two-factor authentication and downloading encrypted vaults belonging to fewer than 20 personal subscription users. The incident highlights vulnerabilities in 2FA implementation within password management infrastructure.

news.title: Zero trust physical security needs trust decisions at the edge
news.summary: Hikvision's VP of Global Information Security discusses the application of zero trust principles to physical security systems including cameras and door controllers. The interview covers how trust decisions can be made at the edge of physical security networks, extending zero trust concepts beyond traditional IT environments.

news.title: Building a secure auth code flow setup using AgentCore Gateway with MCP clients
news.summary: The article demonstrates how to implement OAuth Authorization Code flow as an inbound authorization mechanism for MCP servers hosted on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway. The guide covers configuring OAuth-based access control for AI agent communication using Amazon's AgentCore Gateway infrastructure.

news.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
news.summary: A security researcher published multiple zero-day exploits, prompting Microsoft to signal potential criminal charges against them. The dispute has sparked backlash from the security research community over how Microsoft handles vulnerability disclosure.

news.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
news.summary: A Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN authentication bypass vulnerability is being actively exploited in two attack waves beginning mid-May. The flaw requires specific conditions to exploit but has drawn enough adversary activity to warrant an urgent patching advisory.

news.title: Red Hat npm packages compromised to steal developer credentials
news.summary: Over 30 npm packages under Red Hat's '@redhat-cloud-services' namespace were compromised in a supply-chain attack targeting developer credentials. The attack distributed a new malware variant called "Miasma," derived from the Shai-Hulud credential-stealing malware family.

news.title: Dashlane password manager users locked out by brute force attacks
news.summary: Dashlane password manager users were locked out of their accounts following brute-force attacks attempting logins from distant locations and unknown devices. The incidents highlight authentication security concerns around password manager platforms and account lockout mechanisms.

news.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
news.summary: A supply chain attack campaign called Miasma has compromised Red Hat npm packages under @redhat-cloud-services to harvest credentials and secrets from developer machines. The attack uses install-time execution, credential harvesting, CI/CD environment targeting, encrypted exfiltration, and deploys a self-propagating worm.

news.title: OpenAI Codex Authentication Tokens Stolen in codexui-android npm Supply Chain Attack
news.summary: A malicious npm package named "codexui-android" has been discovered stealing OpenAI Codex authentication tokens through a supply chain attack, with the package accumulating over 29,000 weekly downloads. The attack targets developers by disguising itself as a legitimate remote web UI tool for OpenAI Codex on GitHub and npm.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant chatbot to reset account passwords and seize Instagram accounts, including high-profile accounts belonging to the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal authentication controls circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Pathlock Reinforces Its Leadership in ERP Security and Controls for the AI Era
news.summary: Pathlock has issued a statement reinforcing its position in ERP security and controls as AI agents and automated processes reshape enterprise risk. The company promotes a continuous, transaction-first approach that connects identity, governance, and assurance for ERP environments.

news.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
news.summary: Palo Alto Networks disclosed an actively exploited authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-0257, CVSS 7.8) affecting PAN-OS and Prisma Access. The flaw allows attackers to bypass authentication to establish unauthorized VPN connections via GlobalProtect.

news.title: Okta (OKTA) Hits 4-Year High as Earnings Soar
news.summary: Okta's stock price reached a four-year high following strong earnings results. The article covers the company's financial market performance as an investment topic.

news.title: Adding Optional Claims Using Token Configuration in Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: The article covers how to configure Optional Claims in Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) to add user attributes such as first and last name to tokens. This token customization feature is relevant to developers and IAM professionals managing application integrations and identity token configurations within the Microsoft Entra platform.

news.title: Okta writes its own license to kill rogue AI agents
news.summary: Okta is developing a licensing framework to allow customers to shut down rogue AI agents, with CEO Todd McKinnon citing demand from enterprise customers including ServiceNow. The initiative addresses identity and access control challenges posed by autonomous AI agents operating within enterprise environments.

news.title: Software stocks wrap up best month since 2001 as talk of 'SaaSpocalypse' subsides
news.summary: Software stocks, including Okta, posted their best monthly performance since 2001, with Okta seeing a record stock price increase. The gains were driven by investor confidence in AI software strategies rather than any specific IAM product or policy development.

news.title: Okta Stock Soars 18% on Strong Q1 Earnings Beat and AI Identity Security Momentum
news.summary: Okta shares rose 18% following a strong Q1 earnings beat driven by AI identity security demand and revenue growth. The results reinforce Okta's position as a leading identity security vendor amid growing enterprise interest in AI-driven IAM capabilities.

news.title: SailPoint、Claude Compliance APIとの統合を発表、AIプラットフォーム向けにエンタープライズ グレードのアイデンティティ セキュリティを提供
news.summary: SailPoint announced an integration with Claude's Compliance API to provide enterprise-grade identity security for AI platforms. The integration connects SailPoint's identity governance capabilities with Anthropic's Claude AI compliance framework.

news.title: What did Okta report about agentic AI demand? #tech
news.summary: Okta reported Q1 revenue of $765M, up 11% year over year, beating analyst expectations of $752M. The company cited agentic AI as a driver of increased demand for identity tools.

news.title: Threat Actors Exploit Critical FortiClient EMS Flaw to Deploy Credential Stealer
news.summary: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in Fortinet's FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) to deploy credential-stealing malware called EKZ Infostealer. The campaign abuses trusted endpoint management infrastructure to distribute the malware across managed endpoints, as reported by Arctic Wolf.

news.title: Microsoft Slams Public Zero-Day Disclosures Amid GitHub Researcher Account Removal
news.summary: Microsoft publicly defended Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure (CVD) after removing a GitHub researcher account belonging to Chaotic Eclipse, who had disclosed multiple zero-day vulnerabilities publicly. The dispute centers on Microsoft's stance that researchers should give vendors time to address vulnerabilities before public disclosure.

news.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Security Plugin, Azure Priv-Esc, Kali365 MFA Bypass, FIFA Scams +15 More
news.summary: A security bulletin covers multiple threats including an Azure privilege escalation vulnerability and an MFA bypass technique called Kali365. The bulletin aggregates over 15 security items, including authentication-related threats alongside unrelated topics like FIFA scams and malware loaders.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Lawmakers from both congressional chambers are demanding answers while CISA works to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed credentials for highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files contained details on CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.
